channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: Deadly Israeli strikes hit north and central Gaza
item.description: Israeli airstrikes in northern and central Gaza killed at least 33 people overnight, Palestinian medical officials said Wednesday, as U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Gabbard tests trust of allies as Trump’s potential intel director
item.description: Controversy over President-elect Trump’s pick for intelligence chief, former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), is raising uncomfortable questions for America’s closest allies over whether the U.S. will remain a trusted partner for sharing critical, sensitive information.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Vatican's keffiyeh Nativity scene raises eyebrows and then disappears — at least until Christmas Eve
item.description: One of the Vatican's Nativity scenes raised eyebrows when it was unveiled because it featured a keffiyeh, the Palestinian headscarf.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Syrians flock to morgues looking for loved ones who perished in Assad's prisons
item.description: Many bodies have been found in Syrian detention centers and prisons since President Bashar al-Assad's government fell.
item.dc:creator: Hussein Malla And Omar Albam, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: America's closest ally in Syria is losing ground as a new order takes shape
item.description: The jihadi rebels who toppled Syrian President Bashar Assad say they want to build a unified, inclusive country. But after 14 years of civil war, putting that ideal into practice will not be easy.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US warns experimental Russian missile may be used against Ukraine soon
item.description: Russia could soon launch another attack against Ukraine using an experimental intermediate-range ballistic missile (IRBM), according to a U.S. official. The official said that Russia could fire its Oreshnik IRBM at Ukraine "in the coming days" in an attempt to intimidate Ukraine and its supporters.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Brazil’s Lula is still under intensive care but making progress after surgery, doctors say
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva remains in intensive care in a Sao Paulo hospital but is getting better, doctors said in a statement on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 Ukrainian women are pulled alive from rubble hours after Russian missile strike
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Rescue crews pulled two women from rubble several hours after a Russian missile struck a medical clinic in southern Ukraine, killing nine people and wounding 22 others, authorities said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: An explosion in the Afghan capital kills the Taliban refugee minister
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — A suicide bombing in the Afghan capital on Wednesday killed the Taliban refugee minister and two others, officials said, in the most brazen attack on a member of the Taliban inner circle since they returned to power three years ago.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Macron seeks political deal to name a new prime minister and restore France's stability
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French president Emmanuel Macron is seeking a political deal that would allow him to both name a new prime minister and “guarantee the stability of the country,” following the resignation of ousted Prime Minister Michel Barnier, a spokesperson for the outgoing government said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senators call on Biden to brief upper chamber on efforts to return Austin Tice from Syria
item.description: In a bipartisan push, senators have called on President Biden to brief the upper chamber to provide specific details on efforts underway to secure the release of Austin Tice from captivity in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea condemns South Korea as 'fascist dictatorship' after martial law scare
item.description: North Korean dictator Kim Jong Un denounced South Korea as a 'fascist dictatorship' following President Yoon Suk Yeol's failed attempt to impose martial law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supporters of Bangladesh Nationalist Party hold protest march as tensions continue with India
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Thousands of members of youth and student bodies belonging to the Bangladesh Nationalist Party on Wednesday held a road march procession from Bangladesh’s capital toward its eastern border with India to protest against an attack on a diplomatic mission earlier this month and
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Organized crime gangs expanded into a third of cities in Brazil’s Amazon, report finds
item.description: Criminal gangs are operating in over a third of municipalities in Brazil’s Amazon rainforest driving a boom in violence, according to a report published by the Brazilian Forum of Public Security.
item.dc:creator: Eléonore Hughes, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korea's former defense minister attempted suicide after he was arrested over martial law probe
item.description: South Korea’s former defense minister, Kim Yong Hyun, attempted suicide while in detention over the president's martial law declaration, but officials were able to stop him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli warplanes pound Syria as troops reportedly advance deeper into the country
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Israel carried out a wave of heavy airstrikes across Syria as its troops advanced deeper into the country, a Syrian opposition war monitor said Tuesday, and the Israeli defense minister announced that his forces had destroyed Syria’s navy.
item.dc:creator: ABDULRAHMAN SHAHEEN and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: These Palestinians disappeared after encounters with Israeli troops in Gaza
item.description: Dozens of Palestinians are receiving help from an Israeli legal group in their search for family members who went missing after being separated by Israeli soldiers during raids and arrests in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Sarah El Deeb And Julia Frankel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mike Huckabee 'commends' Biden support for Israel's self-defense
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: US defense secretary in Japan to support alliance as Osprey aircraft safety causes concern
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — U.S. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin met with officials in Japan on Tuesday to reaffirm the importance of their alliance and Washington's commitment to regional security as threats rise from China and North Korea. Austin also stressed that U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syrians in Germany worried by some politicians' eagerness for them to go home after Assad's fall
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Nearly a decade after he arrived in Germany from Syria and took a selfie with then-Chancellor Angela Merkel, Anas Modamani has finished his university studies and has a German passport.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel bombs hundreds of sites across Syria as army pushes into border zone
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours, targeting “most of the strategic weapons stockpiles” in the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Haitians demand protection after gang leader massacres dozens of people to avenge son's death
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — A gang leader accused of killing more than 100 people in Haiti's capital to avenge his son's death was still persecuting residents on Tuesday, according to a rare witness account.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japanese survivor of atomic bomb recalls its horrors in Nobel Peace Prize acceptance speech
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — A 92-year-old Japanese man who lived through the American atomic bombing of Nagasaki described on Tuesday the agony he witnessed in 1945, including the charred corpses of his loved ones and the ruins of his city, as he accepted this year's Nobel Peace Prize on his organization's
item.dc:creator: FANNY BRODERSEN and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Collapse of Syria’s Assad regime renews US push to find Austin Tice
item.description: The fall of the Bashar al-Assad and the mass liberation of numerous notorious prisons in Syria has brought renewed hope for the location and return of American Austin Tice, held captive for 12 years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico chews on Kari Lake ambassador rumors
item.description: Reports of President-elect Trump’s potential nomination of Kari Lake as ambassador to Mexico are receiving mixed reviews in the country, where rumors of the appointment have swirled for weeks. Lake, a hard-line MAGA Republican who lost the open Senate race in Arizona to Sen.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Israel strikes targets in Syria after Assad regime falls
item.description: Israeli forces carried out airstrikes on targets in Syria as Israel continues to take more aggressive action in the country following the collapse of Bashar Assad's regime over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Journalists' rights group says 104 media workers killed this year
item.description: More than half of the journalists and media workers killed perished during the Israel-Hamas war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US hostage envoy in Beirut to seek information on missing journalist Austin Tice
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. government's top hostage negotiator is in Beirut in hopes of collecting information on the whereabouts of Austin Tice, an American journalist missing in Syria for 12 years, the State Department said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel deploys paratroopers to Syria in 'defense activities' after fall of Assad
item.description: Israeli forces released footage of their deployment into Syria following the fall of Bashar Assad's regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu to testify in corruption trial amid multiple conflicts
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will begin his witness testimony Tuesday for his ongoing trial on corruption and fraud charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fall of Syria's Bashar Assad is strategic blow to Iran and Russia, experts say
item.description: Analysts and commentators say the collapse of Syria's Bashar al-Assad's regime is likely to reshape the power dynamics in the Middle East while signaling a critical shift in influence among world powers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Notre Dame opens to the public for first time since 2019 fire
item.description: Later on Sunday, an evening Mass will welcome members of the public who secured reservations last week.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Trump calls for 'immediate ceasefire' in Ukraine after meeting with Zelenskyy
item.description: In a post on his Truth Social platform, Trump claimed that Moscow and Kyiv have both lost hundreds of thousands of soldiers in a war that “should never have started.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says Russia abandoned Syria's Assad, never should have been involved
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said on Sunday that Russia's abandonment of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad led to his downfall.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Syrian insurgents reach gates of Damascus, threatening decades-long Assad regime
item.description: Syrian insurgents have reached the gates of the nation's capital, shortly after Bashar al-Assad's government abandoned the key city of Homs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump is welcomed by Macron to Paris with presidential pomp and joined by Zelenskyy for their talks
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron welcomed Donald Trump to Paris on Saturday with a full dose of presidential pomp as the two held a hastily arranged meeting with Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy before celebrating the grand reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes on a Gaza tent camp kill at least 21 people, hospital says
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes tore through a tent camp for displaced Palestinians in southern Gaza on Wednesday, sparking fires and killing at least 21 people, according to the head of a nearby hospital, in the latest assault on a sprawling tent city that Israel designated a huma
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation
